item.title: Edith Head and Judith Crist celebrate America's bicentennial
item.description: In the lead-up to America's bicentennial in 1976, CBS aired brief segments featuring well-known figures of the time describing moments from early U.S. history. CBS News chief Washington correspondent Major Garrett introduces a pair of clips featuring costume designer Edith Head and movie critic Judith Crist.

item.title: DOJ launches campaign finance probe into Sen. Ruben Gallego, NOTUS reports
item.description: Oriana González, politics reporter for NOTUS, and Julia Manchester, White House correspondent for the Hill, join "The Takeout" to discuss some of the day's biggest political news, including a report from NOTUS that Sen. Ruben Gallego is under a campaign finance investigation.

item.title: Some Russian soldiers last just minutes on front lines, military bloggers say
item.description: Once Russian soldiers reach certain parts of the front lines of the war in Ukraine, they can expect to live an average of just 20 to 35 minutes, according to a grim estimate by Russian military bloggers.

item.title: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed on police funding, Israel, Gaza and his campaign for Senate
item.description: State Sen. Mallory McMorrow, Rep. Haley Stevens and Dr. Abdul El-Sayed are vying for the Democratic nomination for Senate in Michigan. El-Sayed joined "The Takeout" to discuss his campaign.

item.title: Path forward for U.S.-Iran talks unclear after weekend strikes
item.description: Iran denied its negotiators would be meeting with U.S. officials in Qatar after President Trump announced the talks would resume at Tehran's request.

item.title: California lawmaker Scott Wiener says being berated over Gaza "went beyond free speech"
item.description: California congressional candidate and state Sen. Scott Wiener was forced to leave a pro-trans rights march on Friday after being berated by protesters over Gaza. Wiener, who is Jewish, has said he believes Israel's military actions have amounted to genocide. Wiener joined "The Takeout" to discuss the incident.

item.title: Woman killed, 2 injured in series of Florida alligator attacks
item.description: Officials say people and pets should keep a safe distance away from the water to limit the chances of an alligator encounter.

item.title: Death toll from Venezuela earthquakes now over 1,700, thousands still missing
item.description: Rescue teams are still searching for survivors of the deadly earthquakes that struck Venezuela five days ago. More than 1,700 people are confirmed to have died. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more.

item.title: Here's what to know about Medicare's new GLP-1 weight-loss drug program
item.description: For the first time, Medicare will cover GLP-1 drugs prescribed solely for weight loss for eligible beneficiaries at a $50 monthly copay.

item.title: Trump says U.S. and Iran will hold talks Tuesday, Iranians say otherwise
item.description: President Trump said Monday that U.S. and Iranian officials will meet in Doha on Tuesday for more peace talks, but the Iranians are denying that. CBS News' Weijia Jiang has more and Aaron David Miller, senior fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, joins with analysis.

item.title: Breaking down the Supreme Court's decision on mail-in ballots
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday ruled in favor of counting mail-in ballots that are cast by Election Day but arrive later. David Becker, executive director of the Center for Election Innovation and Research, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Takeaways from Supreme Court decisions on presidential firings
item.description: CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford joins "The Takeout" to break down Monday's rulings from the Supreme Court on presidential firings and more.

item.title: Extended interview: Michigan Senate candidate Dr. Abdul El-Sayed
item.description: Dr. Abdul El-Sayed, vying to be the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in Michigan, speaks with Major Garrett about AI, his past comments on police funding, Israel, Gaza and more.

item.title: Millions of women with menopause barred from hormone replacement therapy because of health risk
item.description: Hormone replacement therapy is having a resurgence as more doctors prescribe it to treat menopause symptoms. But millions of women with menopause can't take HRT due to medical reasons, including breast cancer survivors. Dr. Mindy Goldman, chief clinical officer for Midi Health and a clinical professor at UCSF, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: U.S. eases restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos AI model
item.description: The Trump administration is allowing Anthropic to restore access to its Mythos 5 AI model for a select group of U.S. companies and federal agencies. New York Times tech reporter Sheera Frenkel joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Utah mayor on wildfires: "Wrapping our heads around the damage"
item.description: Matt Robinson, the mayor of Beaver, Utah, joins CBS News to discuss the ongoing wildfires that have been burning across the state.

item.title: Watch: Trump takes questions on Supreme Court rulings over mail-in ballots, presidential firings
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters in the Oval Office Monday hours after the Supreme Court handed down landmark decisions on mail-in voting and presidential powers. Mr. Trump also discussed the SAVE Act.

item.title: 6 killed, suspect arrested in shooting at German youth center, police say
item.description: German police say a suspect was in custody and six were killed in a rare shooting that took place at a youth center in Stade, near Hamburg.

item.title: A timeline of the Karen Read case
item.description: A look at the timeline of events in Karen Read's high-profile Massachusetts murder trial and retrial. Read was acquitted of killing her boyfriend, Boston police officer John O'Keefe.

item.title: 3 critical mistakes every saver should avoid this July
item.description: In today's still-elevated interest rate climate, savers need to avoid making these three critical mistakes.

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump bid to fire Fed's Lisa Cook as legal case continues
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Lisa Cook to continue in her post as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors while legal proceedings over President Trump's attempt to fire her continue.

item.title: Archdiocese of San Francisco files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy over child abuse lawsuits
item.description: The Archdiocese of San Francisco is officially filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in the wake of numerous claims of child sexual abuse allegedly committed by priests filed against it, according to an announcement Monday.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Supreme Court expands presidential firing power, overturning 90-year-old ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court overturned a 90-year-old decision that allowed Congress to shield members of certain independent agencies from being fired by the president at will.

item.title: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis indicted in gambling probe
item.description: Former NBA players Malik Beasley and Ed Davis have been indicted on illegal sports gambling charges, authorities announced Monday.

item.title: Comcast plans to split into 2 companies, spinning off NBCUniversal
item.description: Current shareholders would receive shares in both companies under the planned split, Comcast said Monday.

item.title: Rescue teams continue to find survivors of Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Tens of thousands of people are still presumed to be missing after two powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela last week.

item.title: 12-year-old injured by bison at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: A 12-year-old was injured by a bison while visiting Yellowstone National Park last week, the National Park Service said.

item.title: Soccer star's wife and 2 children killed in Venezuela earthquakes, team says
item.description: The wife and two children of Argentine soccer star Lucas Trejo died after powerful earthquakes struck Venezuela, his team said.

item.title: Pablo Escobar murder victim's son is named Colombia's interior minister
item.description: The incoming minister's father, Rodrigo Lara Bonilla, was serving as justice minister in 1984 when he was gunned down in Bogota on Pablo Escobar's orders.
